claims.summary: High-fill stale-window behavior may retire semantic structure before a complex narrative settles.
claims.disposition: Verified current nonlinear stale-window and smooth recovery source without claiming the configured duration is experientially sufficient.

claims.summary: Narrative retention needs a review surface distinct from live stale-window control.
claims.disposition: Verified the read-only narrative semantic retention review and its explicit non-control authority.

claims.summary: High-fill coherence, narrative persistence, and shadow decay should be observed together.
claims.disposition: Routed a bounded temporal trace using existing retention and shadow evidence without altering packet lifetime.

claims.summary: Contextually important narrative should receive a longer stale window.
claims.disposition: Preserved as Tier 5 because importance-based extension changes live sensory persistence and admission policy.
